phases.name: Completion-class legend toggle (US-5144-01)
phases.description: Slice 01 (the whole feature). Add a click-to-toggle Completed/Ongoing legend to the shipped CumulativeStateTimeChart by REUSING the in-house LegendChip + useTypesVisibility mechanic, gated on a new completionFilterEnabled prop; then wire BaseMetricsView to enable it only when no work item is picked. Drives the 7 component acceptance scenarios (CumulativeStateTimeChart.test.tsx) plus a host-wiring test (BaseMetricsView.test.tsx).

steps.name: CumulativeStateTimeChart Completed/Ongoing legend toggle via LegendChip + useTypesVisibility
criteria: When completionFilterEnabled is true, the chart renders two LegendChip chips labelled 'Completed' and 'Ongoing', both active by default; both stacked series are passed to <BarChart> (scenario 1 / AC a)

Clicking the 'Completed' chip removes the completed series from the series passed to <BarChart> (ongoing-only); aria-pressed on that chip becomes false; the value axis rescales to the remaining series (scenario 2 / AC b)
Clicking the 'Ongoing' chip removes the ongoing series (completed-only) (scenario 3 / AC c)
Re-clicking a hidden chip restores its series (scenario 4 / AC d)
At least one segment always remains visible: with one already hidden, clicking the other is a no-op (useTypesVisibility last-visible guard) (scenario 5 / D8 guard)
When completionFilterEnabled is false (or omitted), NO chips render and BOTH series are always passed to <BarChart> (scenario 6 / AC e)
Toggling Completed/Ongoing issues no network request: the chart filters the already-supplied series prop; it does not fetch (D7 guardrail)

All 7 scenarios in the describe.skip('...US-5144-01') block are un-skipped one at a time and green; the existing CumulativeStateTimeChart tests remain green; Biome zero warnings; tsc clean for the touched files
steps.implementation_notes: EXEMPT FROM PARADIGM: React/TypeScript + Vitest project (the TS row of the polyglot matrix governs, not the Python/Hypothesis pilot); Hypothesis/assert_state_delta do NOT apply. These are component-level acceptance tests authored by DISTILL (describe.skip block already present) — DELIVER un-skips them one at a time, it does NOT re-author them. RED handoff already verified by DISTILL: un-skipped, 6/7 fail MISSING_FUNCTIONALITY ('Unable to find button "Completed visibility toggle"'), 1/7 passes as the disabled-filter invariant. REUSE, do not build new (D8): import LegendChip from './LegendChip' and useTypesVisibility from '../../../hooks/useChartVisibility' — the SAME mechanic CycleTimeScatterPlotChart.tsx (lines 268-273) uses for work-item types. Call useTypesVisibility(['Completed','Ongoing']) -> { visibleTypes, toggleTypeVisibility }; the hook already prevents hiding the last visible type (scenario 5 needs no extra code). Render the two LegendChip chips in the existing header Stack (beside pickerSlot) ONLY when completionFilterEnabled — Completed color theme.palette.primary.main, Ongoing color theme.palette.primary.light (representative of the hatch). Build the series array, then pass only series whose visibleTypes[label] !== false to <BarChart> (keep the stable label keys 'Completed'/'Ongoing' for visibility lookup; the BarChart series label may still carry the unit suffix). When completionFilterEnabled is false: render no chips and ALWAYS pass both series; ALSO ensure visibility resets to both-on for the next enable (scenario 7 is covered by step 01-02's rerender path, but the chart must not leak hide-state while disabled). Existing test mock: <BarChart> is mocked to expose data-series as JSON; chips are queried via getByRole('button', { name: 'Completed visibility toggle' }) per LegendChip's aria-label. Keep the existing hidden display:none tooltip Box unchanged (test-only, never visible — D7). IMMUTABILITY: derive the filtered series with .filter/.map, no mutation; max 2 nesting, early returns, no comments. TS strict, no any. CI-LEARNINGS / Biome pre-apply: no unused imports/vars (the SurveyNudge tsc TS6133 is a PRE-EXISTING unrelated file — do NOT touch it); useMemo-stabilise any derived array fed to a hook dep to avoid the React #185 inline-new-object loop (project_react185 memory); pnpm biome check ./src must be clean. Conventional commit 'feat(metrics): ...' with trailer 'Step-ID: 01-01'. POST-GREEN wiring smell check: the chips + filtering are exercised by the un-skipped ATs (not test-only code).

steps.name: Wire BaseMetricsView to enable the completion toggle only when no work item is selected
criteria: BaseMetricsView passes completionFilterEnabled={selectedItemIds.length === 0} to <CumulativeStateTimeChart> so the chips appear only when the item picker has no selection (AC e/f)
When an item is selected (selectedItemIds non-empty) the chart receives completionFilterEnabled=false; clearing the selection restores it to true (AC f reset path)
A Vitest test on BaseMetricsView asserts the wiring (chart receives completionFilterEnabled reflecting selection state) so the host wiring cannot ship Tested-But-Unwired (Sentinel TBU finding)
Behaviour is identical on team and portfolio detail (same component + same wiring) (AC g)
Full frontend suite green; Biome zero warnings; tsc clean for the touched files
steps.scenario_name: passes completionFilterEnabled to the cumulative chart reflecting item-picker selection state
steps.implementation_notes: EXEMPT FROM PARADIGM: React/TypeScript + Vitest project (TS row governs); Hypothesis/assert_state_delta do NOT apply. This step closes the TBU gap Sentinel flagged: the chips are useless unless BaseMetricsView gates them on the picker. WIRING: in the stateTimeCumulative widget render (BaseMetricsView.tsx around line 927-938 where <CumulativeStateTimeChart> is rendered with data + onBarClick + pickerSlot), add completionFilterEnabled={ctx.cumulativeStateTimeSelectedItemIds.length === 0} (the selectedItemIds already flow through ctx — see cumulativeStateTimeSelectedItemIds and the host state at ~line 1049/1284). Do NOT change the item picker behaviour, the narrowed fetch, or displayedCumulativeStateTime. TEST: extend BaseMetricsView.test.tsx — render the view, assert the chart gets completionFilterEnabled=true with no selection; simulate a picker selection (selectedItemIds non-empty) and assert it becomes false; clearing restores true. Reuse the existing BaseMetricsView test harness/mocks (do NOT mock fetch directly; mock the abstractions the suite already mocks). Port-to-port: the assertion goes through the rendered chart's received prop, not internal state. PORTFOLIO PARITY (AC g): BaseMetricsView backs both team and portfolio detail, so one wiring covers both; no portfolio-specific code. CI-LEARNINGS / Biome pre-apply: no unused imports/vars; do NOT introduce an inline new object/array into a useEffect dep (React #185); keep <=2 nesting; no comments; TS strict no any; pnpm biome check ./src clean; pnpm build zero warnings (note: a PRE-EXISTING unrelated TS6133 in SurveyNudge.cadence.test.tsx is out of scope — do not fix or be blocked by it; build the frontend test/typecheck scoped to touched files if needed). Conventional commit 'feat(metrics): ...' with trailer 'Step-ID: 01-02'.

steps.name: Review revision: align completion chips on the left next to the chart title
criteria: The Completed/Ongoing LegendChip chips render on the LEFT of the chart header, immediately after the 'Cumulative Time per State' title, not on the right next to the item picker
The item picker (pickerSlot) stays right-aligned (header keeps justify-content space-between)
All existing CumulativeStateTimeChart tests stay green (they assert chip presence + aria-pressed + series, not position — no test change required); Biome clean; tsc clean for the touched file
steps.scenario_name: existing US-5144-01 scenarios remain green after the layout move (presentational-only)
steps.implementation_notes: EXEMPT FROM PARADIGM: React/TypeScript + Vitest. PRESENTATIONAL-ONLY revision from user slice review (chips should sit next to the title on the left). In CumulativeStateTimeChart.tsx header Stack (~lines 192-221): group the <Typography variant="h6">Cumulative Time per State</Typography> and the completionFilterEnabled chip block together inside a left-side <Stack direction="row" spacing={1} sx={{ alignItems: "center" }}>, and move {pickerSlot} OUT to be the second child of the outer space-between Stack so it stays right-aligned. Set the outer Stack alignItems to "center". No behavior change: chip toggle logic, series filtering, visibleTypes, and props are untouched. No test change expected — the existing 17 scenarios assert chip presence/aria-pressed/series, not DOM position, and must stay green. RED PHASE: no new test is required (presentational, behavior-invariant); record RED as SKIPPED with prefix NOT_APPLICABLE (presentational layout move; existing scenarios already cover the chips and must remain green) and verify the existing tests are GREEN before/after. CI-LEARNINGS / Biome: no comments, <=2 nesting, no unused vars; pnpm biome check clean; do not touch SurveyNudge. Conventional commit 'refactor(metrics): align cumulative completion chips left of the title' with trailer 'Step-ID: 01-03'.

validation.approved_by: orchestrator (proportionate self-validation: 2-step slice, all 7 DISTILL scenarios map to 01-01, host wiring + TBU test in 01-02; schema verified via des-verify-integrity --roadmap-only)
